title: Banana Protein Muffins
tagline: Banana-chocolate protein muffins baked in cupcake moulds in the OTG.
macroNotes: Estimated per muffin (6 muffins from the full batter).
Genuinely gym-friendly — about 7 g protein for ~110 kcal per muffin, low fat. Two muffins make a solid ~14 g protein snack.
Protein boost is already built in via the scoop of whey; for more, use a heaped scoop or add 1 tbsp of a second protein source — but don't overdo protein powder or the muffins turn rubbery.
Add 1 tsp oil or peanut butter to the batter for much better texture; it adds only a few kcal per muffin and stops the protein powder drying them out.

items: 2 medium bananas (ripe is best; soften firm/unripe ones first)
1 tsp honey, sugar or jaggery (helps if bananas aren't very ripe)
1 tsp oil OR peanut butter (optional but improves texture)
3 tbsp flour (atta or maida) — use 3 tbsp max, not 4, so they don't dry out
A few chocolate chips or chopped nuts, to sprinkle

steps.title: Fix firm bananas
steps.body: If your bananas are yellow-but-firm rather than very soft, peel and slice them, microwave 60–90 seconds, then mash — this softens and sweetens them so the muffins don't come out dry. Mash to a smooth, lump-free paste like baby food; visible banana chunks cause wet pockets and gummy muffins.

steps.title: Whisk the wet
steps.body: In a bowl, whisk the mashed banana with 1 egg until smooth. Mix in 3 tbsp milk, 1 tsp honey/sugar/jaggery and 1 tsp oil or peanut butter.

steps.title: Add the dry
steps.body: Add 3 tbsp flour, 1 scoop protein powder, 1–2 tbsp cocoa powder, 1/2 tsp baking powder and a pinch of salt. Mix to a thick pancake-batter consistency — protein powder soaks up a lot of liquid, so if it's too thick add 1 tbsp milk; too runny, add 1 tbsp flour. Don't overmix.

steps.title: Preheat the OTG
steps.body: Preheat the OTG to 180°C for 10 minutes on bake mode (top + bottom rods on).

steps.title: Fill the moulds
steps.body: Lightly grease 6 cupcake moulds and fill each only 70–75% full (about 3–4 tbsp batter each) — protein batter still expands, and overfilling causes overflow. Sprinkle a few chocolate chips or nuts on top if using. Any leftover batter can go into a half-filled microwave-safe mug.

steps.title: Bake
steps.body: Bake on the middle rack at 180°C for 15–18 minutes. Start checking at 15 min: they're done when the tops look matte (not shiny), the edges pull slightly from the mould, and a toothpick comes out with moist crumbs (not wet batter).

steps.title: Cool before removing
steps.body: Let them cool in the moulds 10 minutes before removing — hot protein muffins break easily and firm up as they cool. Expect a soft, slightly dense, brownie-muffin texture, not fluffy bakery cupcakes — that's normal with protein powder.

notes: Air-fryer version: 160°C for ~12–15 min. Microwave version (6 small): fill 60–70%, cook on HIGH starting at 2 min, then 30-sec bursts to 2.5–3.5 min — texture is spongier/steamed and less browned; rest 5–10 min after.

Leftover-batter mug: fill a ceramic mug half full, stir once to redistribute moisture, microwave on HIGH ~1.5 min then 20–30 sec bursts (2–2.5 min total). It comes out like a fudgy banana protein brownie.

Biggest mistakes: too much protein powder (rubbery), not enough banana (dry), overfilling (overflow), overbaking (dry). A smooth banana mash and a moist-crumb toothpick test are the two things that matter most.
source: Adapted from my own cooking notes.
